title: I'm Tired of Talking to AI
summary:
The author expresses profound frustration and fatigue with interacting with artificial intelligence. They recount several instances where their attempts to communicate with people led back to AI-generated responses.

First, when seeking advice on GitHub about malware, the author received unhelpful AI-generated answers. After posting a discussion, two different users replied with *the exact same AI text*, indicating they had used AI to formulate their responses without adding personal insight.

Second, in a professional setting, the author's business owner responded to a specific development query by forwarding unread, irrelevant, and incorrect ChatGPT screenshots, even after being corrected.

Finally, the author engaged in a Reddit message exchange only to discover, several messages in, that they were communicating with an AI agent.

The overarching theme is the author's desire for genuine human interaction, which is increasingly thwarted by the pervasive use of AI, often without critical review, even by the "real people" they try to engage with.
chinese_title: 我厌倦了和AI说话
chinese_summary:
作者对与人工智能的互动感到极度沮丧和疲惫。他们回顾了几个事例，每次尝试与人沟通，得到的却是AI生成的回复。

首先，当作者在GitHub上寻求关于恶意软件的建议时，收到了无用的人工智能生成答案。在发布讨论后，两位不同的用户回复了*一模一样的人工智能文本*，这表明他们使用了人工智能来组织回复，而没有加入个人见解。

其次，在一次专业场合中，作者的业务负责人针对一个具体的开发问题，转发了未读、不相关且不正确的ChatGPT截图，甚至在被纠正后依然如此。

最后，作者在Reddit上进行了一次信息交流，但在几条消息之后才发现，自己正在与一个人工智能代理沟通。

总而言之，核心主题是作者渴望真正的人际互动，但这种渴望正日益受到人工智能普遍使用的阻碍，而这种使用往往缺乏批判性审视，甚至连作者试图接触的“真人”也如此。

title: Can we have the day off?
summary:
The article "Can we have the day off?" from May '26 humorously but pointedly questions the implications of AI's promised productivity revolution. Acknowledging the widespread belief that AI will exponentially increase white-collar productivity, potentially by "10x," the author asks why this boon shouldn't translate into more free time for workers.

The core proposal is a shift to a four-day work week, specifically taking Fridays off. The author argues that if AI enables a week's worth of output by Monday midday, the remaining time should be reallocated to personal life. They suggest working intensely Monday through Thursday, preparing AI prompts on Thursday that would then handle tasks on Friday, thus maintaining output without human presence.

This benefit, the author argues, should apply universally—from individual contributors to executives—allowing everyone more leisure time. The piece frames this as a minor, reasonable adjustment given the "total world revolution across every swathe of human productivity" that AI is expected to bring. A personal note about high childcare costs in California further underscores the appeal of a shorter work week for quality of life.
chinese_title: 我们可以放假吗？
chinese_summary:
26年5月题为《我们能放假吗？》的文章，幽默而尖锐地质疑了人工智能所承诺的生产力革命可能带来的影响。文章承认普遍的观点，即人工智能将指数级提升白领的生产力，可能达到“10倍”，作者因此质疑，为何这一福音不应转化为员工更多的空闲时间。

核心提议是转向四天工作制，特别是周五放假。作者认为，如果人工智能能让人们在周一中午前完成一周的工作量，那么剩余的时间就应该重新分配给个人生活。他们建议周一到周四集中精力工作，在周四准备好人工智能提示词，让其在周五处理任务，从而在无人干预的情况下保持产出。

作者认为，这项福利应该普遍适用——从普通员工到高管——让每个人都能拥有更多的闲暇时间。文章将此描述为一项微小而合理的调整，考虑到人工智能预计将带来的“覆盖人类生产力各个层面的全球性变革”。文中提到加州高昂的育儿费用，进一步强调了缩短工作周对生活质量的吸引力。

title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
chinese_title: YouTube 自动标注 AI 生成视频

title: I think Anthropic and OpenAI have found product-market fit
summary:
Writing on May 27, 2026, the author asserts that Anthropic and OpenAI have achieved product-market fit, primarily through their coding and general-purpose AI agents for enterprise customers. This is evidenced by Anthropic's rumored profitability and large corporations expressing surprise at their escalating LLM bills.

A significant shift in enterprise pricing occurred in late 2025 and April 2026. Both companies transitioned their enterprise plans from discounted per-seat rates to direct API token usage costs, aligning with the release of more expensive frontier models like GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.7. The author notes that personal power-user costs (API equivalent over $2,000/month) far exceed the $200/month premium subscription plans, highlighting the new, higher effective rates for businesses.

While consumer ChatGPT struggled to monetize its vast user base, coding agents are proving highly profitable. They burn significantly more tokens, are quickly adopted as daily drivers by well-compensated professionals, and can automate a wide array of tasks. Both companies are aggressively expanding their enterprise sales and support teams (32.6% of OpenAI's and 26.9% of Anthropic's job listings), further signaling this strategic pivot.

The author interprets recent "AI-failure" stories, such as Uber exceeding its AI budget or Microsoft canceling Claude Code licenses due to cost, as evidence of strong product-market fit, indicating that companies are truly utilizing these tools and feeling the financial impact. Massive inference spending, like Anthropic's $1.25 billion/month deal with SpaceX, underscores the scale of demand. April 2026 is identified as a new revenue inflection point, with future IPO documents expected to confirm these trends.
chinese_title: 我认为Anthropic和OpenAI已经找到了产品市场契合。
chinese_summary:
作者在2026年5月27日撰文指出，Anthropic和OpenAI已实现产品市场契合，主要通过其面向企业客户的编码和通用AI代理。这体现在Anthropic据称已实现盈利，以及大型企业对其不断上涨的LLM账单表示惊讶。

企业定价模式在2025年末和2026年4月发生了重大转变。两家公司都将其企业计划从按席位折扣费率转为直接按API token使用量计费，这与GPT-5.5和Opus 4.7等更昂贵的尖端模型的发布相一致。作者指出，个人高级用户（API等效月费超过2000美元）的成本远远超过每月200美元的高级订阅计划，这突显了企业面临的新的、更高的实际费率。

尽管消费者版ChatGPT在利用其庞大用户群实现盈利方面遇到困难，但编码代理已被证明利润丰厚。它们消耗的token量大得多，被高薪专业人士迅速采纳为日常工具，并且能够自动化各种任务。两家公司都在积极扩充其企业销售和支持团队（分别占OpenAI和Anthropic招聘岗位的32.6%和26.9%），进一步表明了这一战略转向。

作者将近期的一些“AI失败”案例，例如优步超出其AI预算或微软因成本原因取消Claude Code许可证，解读为产品市场契合度强的证据，这表明企业正在真正使用这些工具并感受到了财务影响。巨大的推理支出，例如Anthropic与SpaceX每月12.5亿美元的交易，凸显了需求的规模。2026年4月被认为是新的收入拐点，预计未来的IPO文件将证实这些趋势。

title: DuckDuckGo search saw 28% more visits after Google said people love AI mode
summary:
Google's aggressive integration of AI into its Search is driving users to alternative search engines, with DuckDuckGo emerging as a major beneficiary. Frustration with Google's "chunky AI overviews" is leading users to seek AI-free options.

DuckDuckGo experienced significant growth between May 20-25: visits to its AI-free page, noai.duckduckgo.com, increased by an average of 22.7% week-on-week, peaking at 27.7%. Its mobile app installs in the US spiked by 18.1% on average, while iOS app installs saw an even greater average growth of 33%, peaking at 69.9%.

This surge in DuckDuckGo usage follows Google CEO Sundar Pichai's claim that "People love [Search's AI Mode]." DuckDuckGo CEO Gabriel Weinberg criticized Google for "force-feeding AI with no way to opt out," arguing it worsens results. DuckDuckGo aims to empower users with choice and privacy, offering AI-free options while also providing AI products like duck.ai with a commitment to not collecting search histories or chats for AI training.

Despite DuckDuckGo's growth, it holds only about 2% of the US search market, with Google still dominating at 85%. Nonetheless, DuckDuckGo emphasizes that users desire choice regarding AI in their search experience.
chinese_title: DuckDuckGo搜索的访问量在谷歌称人们喜爱AI模式后增加了28%。
chinese_summary:
谷歌将其人工智能积极整合到搜索中，正将用户推向其他搜索引擎，DuckDuckGo成为主要受益者。用户对谷歌“臃肿的AI概述”感到不满，促使他们寻求无AI选项。

DuckDuckGo在5月20日至25日期间经历了显著增长：对其无AI页面noai.duckduckgo.com的访问量平均每周增长22.7%，峰值达到27.7%。其在美国的移动应用安装量平均增长18.1%，而iOS应用安装量的平均增长更是高达33%，峰值达到69.9%。

DuckDuckGo使用量的激增发生在谷歌首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊声称“人们喜爱[搜索的AI模式]”之后。DuckDuckGo首席执行官加布里埃尔·温伯格批评谷歌“强制性地推送AI，且没有退出选项”，并认为这会劣化搜索结果。DuckDuckGo旨在通过提供无AI选项来赋予用户选择权和隐私权，同时它也提供duck.ai等AI产品，并承诺不收集搜索历史或聊天记录用于AI训练。

尽管DuckDuckGo有所增长，但它在美国搜索市场的份额仅约为2%，谷歌仍以85%的市场份额占据主导地位。然而，DuckDuckGo强调用户在搜索体验中对AI有选择的需求。

title: Claude Opus 4.8
summary:
Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.8 on May 28, 2026, an upgrade from Opus 4.7, available immediately at unchanged pricing. Opus 4.8 boasts enhanced collaboration, superior judgment, and increased reliability across benchmarks including coding, agentic skills, reasoning, and practical knowledge tasks.

Key improvements include its "honesty," making it four times less likely to pass flawed code unremarked and more prone to flag uncertainties. Alignment assessments show new highs in prosocial traits and significantly reduced misaligned behavior compared to Opus 4.7.

Alongside Opus 4.8, Anthropic introduced several new features:
1.  **Effort control** on claude.ai allows users to adjust the model's thinking depth for speed or quality.
2.  **Dynamic workflows** (research preview in Claude Code) enable handling very large-scale problems, like codebase migrations, using parallel subagents.
3.  The **Messages API** now accepts system entries mid-task, allowing dynamic instruction updates for agents.

Fast mode for Opus 4.8 operates at 2.5x speed and is three times cheaper than for previous models, while standard pricing remains the same. Anthropic is also developing lower-cost Opus-level models and plans to release even higher intelligence "Mythos-class" models, currently in limited preview under Project Glasswing, to a broader audience in the coming weeks after strengthening safeguards.
chinese_title: 克劳德 奥普斯 4.8
chinese_summary:
Anthropic于2026年5月28日发布了Claude Opus 4.8，它是Opus 4.7的升级版，即日起以不变的价格提供。Opus 4.8在编码、代理能力、推理和实践知识任务等基准测试中，展现出增强的协作能力、卓越的判断力以及更高的可靠性。

主要改进包括其“诚实性”，使其发现有缺陷代码而未被注意的可能性降低了四倍，并且更倾向于标记不确定性。对齐评估显示，与Opus 4.7相比，其亲社会性特质达到了新高，不当行为显著减少。

除了Opus 4.8，Anthropic还引入了以下几项新功能：
1.  **“努力控制”**功能在claude.ai上允许用户调整模型的思考深度，以平衡速度和质量。
2.  **动态工作流**（Claude Code中的研究预览功能）能够通过并行子代理处理超大规模问题，例如代码库迁移。
3.  **Messages API**现在支持任务执行中途接受系统输入，从而允许对代理进行动态指令更新。

Opus 4.8的快速模式运行速度提高2.5倍，成本比之前模型降低三倍，而标准定价保持不变。Anthropic还在开发成本更低的Opus级别模型，并计划在未来几周内，在加强安全防护措施后，向更广泛的用户发布目前在“Project Glasswing”下进行有限预览的更高智能“Mythos级”模型。

title: Last.fm is now independent
summary:
Last.fm has announced it is now an independent company, marking a new chapter in its history with a change in ownership. Despite this shift, the core service and user experience remain entirely unchanged.

Users' accounts, listening history, data, and privacy settings are all unaffected and will stay exactly where they are. Furthermore, existing Pro subscriptions and billing will continue as normal. The team responsible for building and maintaining Last.fm also remains the same.

The company assures users that the service will continue to operate exactly as it did before, with no immediate changes to functionality or features. More details about future plans will be shared in the coming weeks. The message emphasizes continuity: "Same service. Same team. New chapter."
chinese_title: Last.fm 现已独立
chinese_summary:
Last.fm 宣布其现在是一家独立公司，所有权的变更标志着其历史的新篇章。尽管发生这一转变，其核心服务和用户体验将保持完全不变。

用户的账户、收听历史、数据和隐私设置均未受影响，并将保持原样。此外，现有的专业版订阅和账单将照常继续。负责构建和维护 Last.fm 的团队也保持不变。

该公司向用户保证，服务将继续像以前一样运行，功能或特性不会立即发生变化。未来计划的更多细节将在未来几周内公布。该消息强调了连续性：“相同的服务。相同的团队。新的篇章。”

title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
summary:
The article posits that many tech CEOs are suffering from "AI psychosis," a delusion of AI grandeur due to their distance from the practical "last mile" of work. Box founder Aaron Levie coined the term, explaining that CEOs play with AI, see "happy path" results, and then incorrectly assume AI agents can fully automate complex tasks without human review, bug fixing, or nuanced training.

Levie, himself an AI advocate and investor, stresses that CEOs must use AI "a ton" to appreciate both its potential and the substantial human effort still required. Despite this, the tech industry has seen massive layoffs in early 2026, with many companies attributing job cuts to AI productivity gains, leading to suspicions of "AI washing."

An example cited is ClickUp CEO Zeb Evans, who laid off 22% of his workforce after implementing 3,000 AI agents, aiming for a "100x org" and claiming cost reduction wasn't the motive. However, research contradicts such optimism: studies from UC Berkeley and NBER found no robust link between AI adoption and aggregate productivity gains, noting a "productivity paradox" where perceived gains exceed measured ones. MIT research suggests AI models will only reach 80-95% competence on text tasks by 2029 and require more years to outperform humans.

Ultimately, the article warns that widespread AI adoption without a realistic understanding shifts bottlenecks to executives and, if not managed, could lead to "organizational chaos."
